title: [lichen_l2] iface_init fail path mutex ordering differs from enable path
description:
Cross-file potential deadlock: The fail_late_init cleanup path in lichen_l2_iface_init() only acquires rx_mutex (line 1030), while lichen_l2_enable() acquires tx_mutex THEN rx_mutex (lines 695-696 for enable, 778-779 for disable).

Current safety: During iface_init, the interface is not yet operational - no concurrent TX should be possible since lichen_iface is only set at line 975, and the RX callback is only registered at line 978. So there's no practical race.

Future hazard: If code is refactored to allow early TX (e.g., during boot for neighbor discovery), the inconsistent lock ordering could cause ABBA deadlock between:
- fail_late_init path: rx_mutex only
- concurrent TX: tx_mutex only (or tx_mutex then rx_mutex if refactored)

Location: lichen_l2.c:1028-1034 (fail_late_init) vs lines 695-696, 778-779 (enable)

Recommendation: For consistency, fail_late_init should acquire tx_mutex then rx_mutex, matching lichen_l2_enable(). Even if currently safe, consistent lock ordering prevents future bugs.
close_reason: Fixed: fail_late_init path now acquires both tx_mutex and rx_mutex in the correct order (tx_mutex first, then rx_mutex), matching the enable/disable paths in lichen_l2_enable(). Updated comment to document the lock ordering rationale.
